resources.string: Pro First Aid
Injuries
Life Threatening
How to stop life-threatening blood loss.
Treating heat or chemical burns.
Helping someone who cannot breathe.
For adults who are unresponsive.
Emergency Call
Search emergencies...
Apply direct pressure with a clean cloth or gauze.
Do not remove soaked bandages; add more on top.
If bleeding is from a limb and severe, use a tourniquet.
Stop the burning by removing the heat source.
Cool the burn with cool running water (not ice).
Cover loosely with a sterile, non-stick dressing.
Stand behind the person and wrap your arms around the waist.
Make a fist and place the thumb side just above the navel.
Grasp your fist with your other hand.
Perform quick, upward and inward thrusts (Heimlich maneuver).
Check the scene for safety and the person for responsiveness.
Call 911 (or local emergency number) immediately.
Place the person on their back on a firm, flat surface.
Give 30 chest compressions (2 inches deep, 100-120 bpm).
Give 2 rescue breaths (tilt head, lift chin, pinch nose).
Repeat cycles of 30 compressions and 2 breaths.
Severe Bleeding
Burns
Choking
CPR
